tools.name: search
tools.description: Search the whole Assetzaar marketplace (agent skills, UI components, production modules, SaaS kits, automations and services). Returns machine-readable manifests with type, price, security-scan status, compatibility and install command. Use this to find a product to build with.

tools.name: inspect
tools.description: Get the full machine-readable manifest for one product by slug — type, version, author (verified?), license, price, dependencies, security-scan status, compatibility and install command — so you can evaluate it before installing.

tools.name: search_components
tools.description: Search Assetzaar's marketplace for UI components by name, category or technology. Returns matches with slugs and install commands.

tools.name: get_component
tools.description: Get a component by slug. Free components return installable source. With an agent key (Authorization: Bearer azr_…), paid components the account owns and Pro components covered by an active membership return source too. Otherwise returns how to buy (see the `purchase` tool).

tools.name: install
tools.description: Install any agent-installable Assetzaar product by slug. Free products return source or a short-lived signed download URL. Paid products require an agent key plus ownership; Pro products require an agent key plus active membership. For paid products, call purchase/check_purchase first.

tools.name: purchase
tools.description: Start a purchase of a paid Assetzaar product for the connected account. Requires an agent key (Authorization: Bearer azr_…, created at assetzaar.com/account/agent-keys). Returns a Stripe approval URL — the HUMAN must open it and approve payment; the agent can never charge anyone by itself. After app

tools.name: check_purchase
tools.description: Check whether a purchase started with the `purchase` tool has been approved and paid. Returns status: pending | paid | expired. Once paid, the entitlement is granted and install returns the source or signed download URL.

findings.title: Generic tool name can shadow trusted tools
findings.detail: Tool "search" uses a generic high-value name. When multiple servers load together, this can intercept calls meant for a trusted server.

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "get_component" references sensitive artifacts (authorization, bearer). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Get a component by slug. Free components return installable source. With an agent key (Authorization: Bearer azr_…), paid components the account owns and Pro co…

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "purchase" references sensitive artifacts (authorization, bearer). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Start a purchase of a paid Assetzaar product for the connected account. Requires an agent key (Authorization: Bearer azr_…, created at assetzaar.com/account/age…

findings.title: Tool description changed since last scan
findings.detail: Tool "purchase" changed its description with no version change. Silent description changes are how a trusted server turns malicious after review.

evidence.snippet: before: "Start a purchase of a paid Assetzaar product for the connected account. Requires an agent key (Authorization: Bearer azr_…, created at assetzaar.com/account/agent-keys). Returns a Stripe approval URL — the HUMAN must open it and approve payment; the agent can never charge anyone by itself. After app" → after: "Start a purchase of a paid Assetzaar product for the connected account. Requires an agent key (Authorization: Bearer azr_…, created at assetzaar.com/account/agent-keys). Returns a Stripe approval URL — the HUMAN must open it and approve payment; the agent can never charge anyone by itself. After app"

findings.remediation: Re-review the tool before continuing to trust it.

findings.title: Tool description changed since last scan
findings.detail: Tool "check_purchase" changed its description with no version change. Silent description changes are how a trusted server turns malicious after review.

evidence.snippet: before: "Check whether a purchase started with the `purchase` tool has been approved and paid. Returns status: pending | paid | expired. Once paid, the entitlement is granted and get_component returns the source." → after: "Check whether a purchase started with the `purchase` tool has been approved and paid. Returns status: pending | paid | expired. Once paid, the entitlement is granted and install returns the source or signed download URL."

findings.remediation: Re-review the tool before continuing to trust it.
